guide.also_known_as: WBC, TLC, Leukocytes, White Cell Count

tests.name: Hemoglobin
tests.low_causes: Your hemoglobin is low — this means your blood may not be carrying enough oxygen around your body. You may feel tired, weak, or short of breath. The most common cause is low iron, often from poor diet or blood loss. See a doctor for a simple iron test to find out why.
tests.high_causes: Your hemoglobin is higher than normal. This can happen due to dehydration or living at high altitude. In some cases it may need further investigation. Please consult your doctor.

tests.name: White Blood Cells
tests.low_causes: Your white blood cell count is low. White blood cells fight infections, so a low count means your immune system may be weaker than usual. This can happen after a viral illness or certain medications. Please see a doctor especially if you feel unwell or have a fever.
tests.high_causes: Your white blood cell count is high. This usually means your body is actively fighting an infection or inflammation somewhere. It is common with bacterial infections. See a doctor if you have fever, pain, or feel generally unwell.

tests.name: Platelet Count
tests.low_causes: Your platelet count is low. Platelets help your blood clot when you bleed. A low count means you may bruise or bleed more easily than normal. This can happen with viral infections or certain medications. Please see a doctor promptly.
tests.high_causes: Your platelet count is higher than normal. This is often a response to inflammation or infection in the body and usually not dangerous on its own. Your doctor can advise if any follow up is needed.

tests.name: Red Blood Cells
tests.low_causes: Your red blood cell count is low. Red blood cells carry oxygen throughout your body. A low count often means you have anemia and may feel tired or dizzy. Please see a doctor to find the cause.
tests.high_causes: Your red blood cell count is higher than normal. This can sometimes be caused by dehydration. Please see your doctor to discuss this result.

tests.name: Hematocrit (PCV)
tests.low_causes: Your hematocrit is low. This means there are fewer red blood cells than normal in your blood and is usually a sign of anemia. You may feel tired or short of breath. Please consult your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your hematocrit is higher than normal. This can be caused by dehydration. Drink plenty of water and consult your doctor if it remains high.

tests.name: Mean Corpuscular Volume (MCV)
tests.low_causes: Your MCV is low, meaning your red blood cells are smaller than normal. This is commonly caused by iron deficiency or a condition called thalassemia. A doctor can order further tests to find out.
tests.high_causes: Your MCV is high, meaning your red blood cells are larger than normal. This is often caused by low Vitamin B12 or folate. It can also happen with certain medications or liver conditions. Please see a doctor.

tests.name: Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin (MCH)
tests.low_causes: Your MCH is low. This means each red blood cell carries less hemoglobin than normal, often due to iron deficiency. Please consult your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your MCH is high. This is often seen alongside a high MCV and may indicate low Vitamin B12 or folate. Please see your doctor.

tests.name: Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin Concentration (MCHC)
tests.low_causes: Your MCHC is low, which means your red blood cells have less color than normal. This is usually a sign of iron deficiency anemia. Please consult your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your MCHC is higher than normal. This can sometimes be a lab finding or may need further investigation. Please discuss with your doctor.

tests.name: Red Cell Distribution Width (RDW)
tests.low_causes: Your RDW is within a low normal range. This is generally not a concern.
tests.high_causes: Your RDW is high. This means your red blood cells vary a lot in size, which can be a sign of mixed nutritional deficiencies like iron and Vitamin B12 together. Please see a doctor for further tests.

tests.name: ALT (SGPT)
tests.low_causes: Your ALT is normal or low. This is generally not a concern.
tests.high_causes: Your ALT is high. ALT is a liver enzyme and when it rises it usually means the liver is under stress or has been injured. Common causes include fatty liver, viral hepatitis, or certain medications. Please see a doctor — this needs follow up.

tests.name: AST (SGOT)
tests.low_causes: Your AST is normal. This is not a concern.
tests.high_causes: Your AST is high. This enzyme rises when the liver or muscles are damaged. Common causes include liver disease or intense physical exertion. Please consult your doctor for further evaluation.

tests.name: Alkaline Phosphatase (ALP)
tests.low_causes: Your ALP is low. This can sometimes be related to poor nutrition. Please mention it to your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your ALP is high. This can be related to the liver, bile ducts, or bones. It is also normally higher during pregnancy. Please see a doctor to find out the cause.

tests.name: Total Bilirubin
tests.low_causes: Your bilirubin is normal. No concern.
tests.high_causes: Your bilirubin is high. Bilirubin is a yellow pigment produced when red blood cells break down. A high level can cause yellowing of the skin or eyes (jaundice). This needs medical attention — please see a doctor soon.

tests.name: Albumin
tests.low_causes: Your albumin is low. Albumin is a protein made by the liver. A low level can be a sign of liver disease, kidney problems, or poor nutrition. Please see a doctor to find out why.
tests.high_causes: Your albumin is slightly high. This is usually caused by dehydration. Make sure you are drinking enough fluids and mention this to your doctor.

tests.name: Blood Urea
tests.low_causes: Your urea is low. This can sometimes happen with low protein intake or liver problems. Please mention it to your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your urea is high. This can be a sign that your kidneys are not filtering waste properly, or it can happen with dehydration or a high protein diet. Please see a doctor for further evaluation.

tests.name: Serum Creatinine
tests.low_causes: Your creatinine is low. This can happen with low muscle mass and is usually not a concern. Mention it to your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your creatinine is high. Creatinine is a waste product filtered by the kidneys. A high level is an important sign that your kidneys may not be working properly. Please see a doctor as soon as possible.

tests.name: Sodium (Na⁺)
tests.low_causes: Your sodium is low. Sodium helps control fluid balance in your body. A low level can cause symptoms like headache, nausea, or confusion. Please see a doctor — this needs attention.
tests.high_causes: Your sodium is high. This is often caused by not drinking enough water. Make sure you stay well hydrated and consult your doctor.

tests.name: Potassium (K⁺)
tests.low_causes: Your potassium is low. Potassium is important for your heart and muscles. A low level can cause muscle weakness or cramps. Please see a doctor — this may need treatment.
tests.high_causes: Your potassium is high. High potassium can affect your heart rhythm. Please see a doctor promptly, especially if you feel any palpitations or muscle weakness.

tests.name: Chloride (Cl⁻)
tests.low_causes: Your chloride is low. This can happen with vomiting or certain medications. Please mention it to your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your chloride is high. This can be a sign of dehydration or a kidney issue. Please consult your doctor.

tests.name: TSH
tests.low_causes: Your TSH is low. This usually means your thyroid gland is overactive and producing too much thyroid hormone. You may feel anxious, have a fast heartbeat, lose weight, or feel hot. Please see a doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your TSH is high. This usually means your thyroid gland is underactive and not producing enough thyroid hormone. You may feel tired, gain weight, feel cold, or have dry skin. Please see a doctor — this is very treatable.

tests.name: Free T3
tests.low_causes: Your Free T3 is low. This can be a sign of an underactive thyroid. Please consult your doctor along with your TSH result.
tests.high_causes: Your Free T3 is high. This can be a sign of an overactive thyroid. Please consult your doctor along with your TSH result.

tests.name: Free T4
tests.low_causes: Your Free T4 is low. This suggests your thyroid may be underactive. Please see a doctor who will likely check your TSH as well.
tests.high_causes: Your Free T4 is high. This suggests your thyroid may be overactive. Please see a doctor for further evaluation.

guide.also_known_as: PCO2, pCO₂, Partial Pressure of CO2
PO2, pO₂, Partial Pressure of Oxygen

tests.name: pH
tests.low_causes: Your blood pH is low, meaning your blood is more acidic than normal. This needs medical attention.
tests.high_causes: Your blood pH is high, meaning your blood is more alkaline than normal. This needs medical attention.

tests.name: pCO₂
tests.low_causes: Your pCO2 is low. This can happen when you are breathing too fast. Please consult your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your pCO2 is high. This can happen when the lungs are not removing enough carbon dioxide. Please consult your doctor.

tests.name: HCO₃⁻
tests.low_causes: Your bicarbonate is low. This can be a sign of metabolic acidosis. Please see a doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your bicarbonate is high. This can be a sign of metabolic alkalosis. Please see a doctor.

tests.name: pO₂
tests.low_causes: Your oxygen level in the blood is low. This means your lungs may not be getting enough oxygen into your bloodstream. Please seek medical attention.
tests.high_causes: Your pO2 is high. This is usually seen when someone is on supplemental oxygen. Generally not a concern.

tests.name: O₂ Saturation
tests.low_causes: Your oxygen saturation is low. This means your blood is not carrying as much oxygen as it should. Below 94% needs medical attention. Please see a doctor or go to the emergency room if you feel breathless.
tests.high_causes: Your oxygen saturation is high. This is usually fine and often seen with supplemental oxygen.

guide.also_known_as: White Blood Cells, Pus Cells, WBC/HPF

tests.name: Color
tests.low_causes: Normal urine color.
tests.high_causes: Abnormal urine color can indicate dehydration (dark yellow), blood in urine (red or pink), or liver problems (brown). Please see a doctor if the color looks unusual.

tests.name: Appearance
tests.low_causes: Clear urine is normal.
tests.high_causes: Cloudy or turbid urine often means there may be an infection, pus cells, or crystals in the urine. Please see a doctor.

tests.name: Specific Gravity
tests.low_causes: Your urine is very dilute. This can happen when you drink a lot of water. Sometimes it can be a sign of diabetes insipidus. Mention it to your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your urine is very concentrated. This usually means you are not drinking enough water. Try to increase your fluid intake and consult your doctor if it persists.

tests.name: pH
tests.low_causes: Your urine is acidic. This can happen with a high protein diet. Usually not a concern.
tests.high_causes: Your urine is alkaline. This can happen with a vegetarian diet or a urinary tract infection. Please mention it to your doctor.

tests.name: Protein
tests.low_causes: No protein in urine. This is normal.
tests.high_causes: Protein was found in your urine. Normally there should be no protein in urine. This can be a sign of kidney problems. Please see a doctor for further testing.

tests.name: Glucose
tests.low_causes: No glucose in urine. This is normal.
tests.high_causes: Glucose was found in your urine. This can be a sign of diabetes or very high blood sugar. Please see a doctor and get your blood sugar checked.

tests.name: Ketones
tests.low_causes: No ketones in urine. This is normal.
tests.high_causes: Ketones were found in your urine. This can happen when you are not eating enough, on a very low carb diet, or in diabetic patients with poorly controlled sugar. Please see a doctor.

tests.name: Bilirubin
tests.low_causes: No bilirubin in urine. This is normal.
tests.high_causes: Bilirubin was found in your urine. This can be a sign of liver disease or a blockage in the bile ducts. Please see a doctor.

tests.name: Urobilinogen
tests.low_causes: Your urobilinogen is low. This can sometimes happen with bile duct problems or antibiotic use. Mention it to your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your urobilinogen is high. This can be a sign of liver disease or increased breakdown of red blood cells. Please see a doctor.

tests.name: RBCs
tests.low_causes: No red blood cells in urine. This is normal.
tests.high_causes: Red blood cells were found in your urine. This means there may be blood in your urine, which can be caused by a urinary tract infection, kidney stones, or other conditions. Please see a doctor.

tests.name: WBCs
tests.low_causes: No significant white blood cells in urine. This is normal.
tests.high_causes: White blood cells were found in your urine. This usually means there is an infection or inflammation in the urinary tract. Please see a doctor.

tests.name: Pus Cells
tests.low_causes: No significant pus cells. This is normal.
tests.high_causes: Pus cells were found in your urine. This is a strong sign of a urinary tract infection. Please see a doctor who may prescribe antibiotics.

tests.name: Casts
tests.low_causes: No casts found. This is normal.
tests.high_causes: Casts were found in your urine. This can be a sign of kidney disease. Please see a doctor for further evaluation.

tests.name: Crystals
tests.low_causes: No significant crystals found. This is normal.
tests.high_causes: Crystals were found in your urine. This can increase the risk of kidney stones. Drink plenty of water and consult your doctor.

tests.name: Bacteria
tests.low_causes: No bacteria found. This is normal.
tests.high_causes: Bacteria were found in your urine. This is a sign of a urinary tract infection. Please see a doctor for appropriate treatment.

tests.name: Epithelial Cells
tests.low_causes: Normal finding.
tests.high_causes: A high number of epithelial cells in urine can sometimes indicate contamination of the sample or an underlying issue. Please mention it to your doctor.

tests.name: Total Cholesterol
tests.low_causes: Your total cholesterol is normal or low. This is generally a good sign for heart health.
tests.high_causes: Your total cholesterol is high. High cholesterol means fatty deposits can build up in your blood vessels over time, increasing the risk of heart disease and stroke. Please see a doctor who can advise on diet changes or medication.

tests.name: LDL Cholesterol
tests.low_causes: Your LDL is low or normal. This is good for your heart health.
tests.high_causes: Your LDL (bad cholesterol) is high. LDL is the type of cholesterol that clogs arteries and increases the risk of heart attack and stroke. This needs attention — please see a doctor to discuss lifestyle changes and possible treatment.

tests.name: HDL Cholesterol
tests.low_causes: Your HDL (good cholesterol) is low. HDL helps remove bad cholesterol from your blood vessels. A low level increases the risk of heart disease. Regular exercise and a healthy diet can help raise it. Please speak to your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your HDL is high. HDL is the good cholesterol that protects your heart. A high level is generally a positive sign.

tests.name: Triglycerides
tests.low_causes: Your triglycerides are normal. This is a good sign.
tests.high_causes: Your triglycerides are high. Triglycerides are a type of fat in the blood. High levels are often related to a diet high in sugar and refined carbohydrates, or excess alcohol. They increase the risk of heart disease. Please see a doctor and consider dietary changes.

tests.name: VLDL Cholesterol
tests.low_causes: Your VLDL is normal or low. This is generally fine.
tests.high_causes: Your VLDL is high. VLDL carries triglycerides in the blood and high levels contribute to fatty buildup in arteries. Please discuss this with your doctor.

guide.also_known_as: FBS, Fasting Glucose, FBG, Fasting Blood Glucose
RBS, Random Glucose, RBG, Blood Sugar Random
Glycated Hemoglobin, A1c, Hemoglobin A1c, HBA1C
PPBS, 2hr Post Meal, Post Meal Glucose

tests.name: Fasting Blood Sugar
tests.low_causes: Your fasting blood sugar is low (hypoglycemia). This can cause dizziness, sweating, or feeling faint. If you feel these symptoms eat something sweet immediately and see a doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your fasting blood sugar is high. A level above 126 mg/dL on two separate tests usually indicates diabetes. Between 100-125 is called prediabetes. Please see a doctor as soon as possible — diabetes is very manageable when caught early.

tests.name: Random Blood Sugar
tests.low_causes: Your random blood sugar is low. If you feel dizzy or faint eat something sweet and see a doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your random blood sugar is high. A level above 200 mg/dL along with symptoms like excessive thirst or urination can indicate diabetes. Please see a doctor for proper diagnosis.

tests.name: HbA1c
tests.low_causes: Your HbA1c is normal. This means your average blood sugar over the past 3 months has been well controlled.
tests.high_causes: Your HbA1c is high. This test shows your average blood sugar over the past 3 months. Above 6.5% usually means diabetes. Between 5.7-6.4% is prediabetes. Please see a doctor — the earlier you manage this the better the outcome.

tests.name: Post Prandial Blood Sugar
tests.low_causes: Your post meal blood sugar is normal. This is a good sign.
tests.high_causes: Your blood sugar is high 2 hours after eating. Normally it should return below 140 mg/dL after a meal. A high reading can be an early sign of diabetes or prediabetes. Please see a doctor.

guide.also_known_as: Total Iron Binding Capacity, Iron Binding Capacity

tests.name: Serum Iron
tests.low_causes: Your serum iron is low. This means there is not enough iron circulating in your blood. You may feel tired, weak, or short of breath. Iron deficiency is very common and very treatable. Please see a doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your serum iron is high. This can happen with certain conditions affecting how the body stores iron. Please see a doctor for further evaluation.

tests.name: Ferritin
tests.low_causes: Your ferritin is low. Ferritin is the stored form of iron in your body. A low level is the earliest sign of iron deficiency, even before anemia develops. You may feel tired or have hair loss. Please see a doctor — iron supplements can help.
tests.high_causes: Your ferritin is high. Very high ferritin can sometimes indicate inflammation, liver disease, or a condition where the body stores too much iron. Please see a doctor for further tests.

tests.name: TIBC
tests.low_causes: Your TIBC is low. This can sometimes indicate inflammation or liver problems. Please mention it to your doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your TIBC is high. A high TIBC alongside low iron and low ferritin is a strong indicator of iron deficiency. Please see a doctor.

tests.name: Transferrin Saturation
tests.low_causes: Your transferrin saturation is low. This means your iron stores are running low. Combined with other iron markers this helps confirm iron deficiency. Please see a doctor.
tests.high_causes: Your transferrin saturation is high. This can be a sign that the body is storing too much iron. Please see a doctor for further evaluation.

guide.also_known_as: Vit D, 25-OH Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxyvitamin D, Calcidiol

tests.name: Vitamin D
tests.low_causes: Your Vitamin D is low. Vitamin D deficiency is extremely common in Pakistan despite the sunny climate because most people avoid direct sun exposure. Low Vitamin D can cause bone pain, muscle weakness, fatigue, and low mood. The good news is it is easily treated with Vitamin D supplements. Please see a doctor for the right dose.
tests.high_causes: Your Vitamin D is very high. This is usually caused by taking too many Vitamin D supplements. Very high levels can cause nausea, weakness, and kidney problems. Please stop supplements and see a doctor.

tests.name: Vitamin B12
tests.low_causes: Your Vitamin B12 is low. B12 deficiency can cause extreme fatigue, tingling or numbness in hands and feet, memory problems, and mood changes. It is common in people who eat little meat or dairy. Please see a doctor — B12 injections or supplements work very well.
tests.high_causes: Your Vitamin B12 is very high. This can sometimes happen with liver disease or certain blood conditions. Please mention it to your doctor.

guide.also_known_as: Hepatitis B Surface Antigen, Hep B Antigen, HBs Ag
Hepatitis C Antibody, HCV Antibody, Anti HCV
Hepatitis B Surface Antibody, HBs Antibody
Hepatitis B e Antigen, Hep B e Ag

tests.name: HBsAg
tests.low_causes: HBsAg is negative. This means you do not currently have a Hepatitis B infection. This is a normal result.
tests.high_causes: HBsAg is positive. This means Hepatitis B virus is present in your blood. This could be a new or long-standing infection. Please see a doctor as soon as possible — Hepatitis B is manageable with the right treatment and monitoring.

tests.name: Anti-HCV
tests.low_causes: Anti-HCV is negative. This means no Hepatitis C antibodies were found. This is a normal result.
tests.high_causes: Anti-HCV is positive. This means your body has been exposed to the Hepatitis C virus at some point. Please see a doctor immediately for a confirmatory test. Hepatitis C is now completely curable with modern medicines available in Pakistan.

tests.name: Anti-HBs
tests.low_causes: Your Anti-HBs is low or negative. This means you may not be fully protected against Hepatitis B. If you have not been vaccinated please speak to your doctor about getting the Hepatitis B vaccine.
tests.high_causes: Your Anti-HBs is positive. This means you have immunity against Hepatitis B — either from vaccination or a past infection that your body cleared. This is a good result.

tests.name: HBeAg
tests.low_causes: HBeAg is negative. This is a normal finding.
tests.high_causes: HBeAg is positive. This marker is checked in people who already have Hepatitis B. A positive result means the virus is actively multiplying and you are more infectious. Please see a liver specialist promptly.
